title: The 1806 Interior Expeditions — Zalvidea & Moraga
subtitle: Two probes into the Central Valley, July–November 1806
abstract: The 1806 expeditions of Fr. José María de Zalvidea (Santa Ynez to San Gabriel through the southern valley) and Gabriel Moraga with Fr. Pedro Muñoz (San Juan Bautista through the San Joaquin Valley), stop by stop from the diaries as identified by Cook. Routes are reconstructed; village locations approximate.

sources.citation: Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

features.name: Pizcache
features.summary: On the San Joaquin, near Le Grand

sources.citation: Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

features.name: Pitkachi
features.summary: South side San Joaquin; Kohou near Herndon

Cook, Colonial Expeditions (village identifications approximate)

routes.label: Zalvidea Expedition (Jul–Aug 1806)
routes.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 1)

stops.name: Mission Santa Ynez
stops.summary: Departure, July 20, 1806.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions

stops.name: Jonatas — Las Olivas
stops.summary: First stop north of mission, at Las Olivas (present-day Los Olivos area).

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 1)

stops.name: Saca — Alamo Pintado Creek
stops.summary: Village of Saca on Alamo Pintado Creek.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 1)

stops.name: Olomosong — Sisquoc River
stops.summary: Probably on the Sisquoc R. near the 120th meridian.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 1: 'After 4 leagues further travel')

stops.name: Geep — South Slope, Sierra Madre
stops.summary: Apparently on the south slope of the Sierra Madre range. After climbing a mountain they came out onto the Cuyama Valley plains. T 10 N, R 28 W.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 1)

stops.name: Talhulimit
stops.summary: Two leagues east of Geep, in the Cuyama Valley.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 1)

stops.name: Lisahua — Salisbury Canyon
stops.summary: Probably in lower Salisbury Canyon. T 9 N, R 26 W.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 2)

stops.name: Camp — Lower Santa Barbara Canyon
stops.summary: In lower Santa Barbara Canyon. T 9 N, R 25 W.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note (cont.))

stops.name: Sgene — Lower Cuyama Valley
stops.summary: Party bore more to the north and found Sgene. T 10 N, R 25 W.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 3)

stops.name: Malapoa — Bitterwater / Santiago Creek
stops.summary: Located by Gifford & Schenck on Bitterwater or Santiago Cr. Identified as Tlamini (Kroeber).

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 4)

stops.name: Sispetis — SE Buena Vista Lake
stops.summary: Considered to be Pohatan Tinliu, at the SE corner of Buena Vista Lake.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 4: Kroeber (1925, pl. 47))

stops.name: Buenasvista — 8 Leagues North
stops.summary: Eight leagues north of Bitterwater/Santiago Cr.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 5)

stops.name: Tejon / El Paso Creek — Foothills
stops.summary: Six leagues from Sispetis to the mouth of Tejon or El Paso Cr. at the edge of the foothills. July 26 reconnaissance explored El Paso, Tejon, and Pastoria creeks.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 6)

stops.name: Tupal — Tejon Ranch on El Paso Creek
stops.summary: Placed doubtfully by Gifford & Schenck at Tejon Ranch on El Paso Cr.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 6)

stops.name: Tacui — Tecuya Creek
stops.summary: Party doubled back west past Grapevine Cr. to Tacui, undoubtedly on Tecuya Cr.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 7)

stops.name: Yagueleme — Kern River / Woilo
stops.summary: Nine leagues north of Tecuya, on the Kern R. Gifford & Schenck identify as Woilo, very close to 9 leagues from Tecuya.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 8)

stops.name: Eastern End of Kern Lake
stops.summary: The eastern end of Kern Lake.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 9)

stops.name: Tashlpj — Pastoria / Grapevine Creek
stops.summary: Cook thinks more likely Pastoria on Grapevine Cr. rather than Tejon Cr., since the party arrived at Castaic at the head of Grapevine Cr. on August 7.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 10)

stops.name: Castaic — Head of Grapevine Creek
stops.summary: Party arrived August 7. Beginning the crossing south.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions

stops.name: Antelope Valley
stops.summary: Crossed into Antelope Valley.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 11)

stops.name: San Gabriel Mountains Crossing
stops.summary: Party crossed the mountains and went southwest. Several villages of Serrano Indians seen.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 12)

stops.name: Mission San Gabriel
stops.summary: Arrival at San Gabriel Mission.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 12)

routes.label: Moraga — Northbound (Sep–Oct 1806)
routes.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions

stops.name: Mission San Juan Bautista
stops.summary: Departure, late September 1806. Fr. Pedro Muñoz accompanying as chaplain.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions

stops.name: San Benito River Camp
stops.summary: 1½ leagues from the mission, on the San Benito R.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 13: 'not on Pacheco Cr., as stated by Cutter')

stops.name: San Luis Ranch — Pacheco Pass
stops.summary: Camp approximately at San Luis Ranch, where Highway 152 crosses San Luis Cr.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 14)

stops.name: Santa Rita Camp
stops.summary: 15 to 20 mi east of San Luis Gonzaga. Near Salt Slough or Paso Slough, 5–6 mi west of the main San Joaquin.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 15: 'The sloughs and channels of the San Joaquin are difficult to locate with precision on a modern map')

stops.name: Camp on the San Joaquin
stops.summary: On the San Joaquin R. T 8 S, R 11 E.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 16: villages effaced so thoroughly that modern informants gave no indication they ever existed)

stops.name: Bear Creek
stops.summary: Rather than Mariposa Cr. Approximately 3 leagues south of the Merced R.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 18: 'Cutter thinks this was Bear Cr... I see no reason to disagree')

stops.name: Merced River — West of Livingston
stops.summary: Party which went north reached the Merced R. somewhere west of Livingston, probably at T 6 S, R 11 E.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 19)

stops.name: Dry Creek
stops.summary: Undoubtedly Dry Cr. 'The description is valid even today.'

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 21)

stops.name: Tuolumne River Area
stops.summary: Villages seen on the Tuolumne but were deserted. Oak forest environment.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook notes 20, 24: Taulames village between the Tuolumne and Stanislaus)

stops.name: Stanislaus River — Near Modesto
stops.summary: Remnants of the oak forest can still be seen, extending from the junction with the San Joaquin eastward. Both sides of the river.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 22: 'if it continued in a northwesterly direction from near Modesto')

stops.name: Upper Stanislaus — Knights Ferry Area
stops.summary: Six leagues upstream. Vicinity of Ripon, Riverbank, or Knights Ferry. Limestone bluffs, precipitous. The best guess is the vicinity of Knights Ferry.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 23)

stops.name: Calaveras River
stops.summary: First stream (Rio de ... Franciscos) identified as the Calaveras.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 25: 'There is little reason to disagree')

stops.name: Cosumnes River — Northernmost Point
stops.summary: Second stream identified as the Cosumnes. Linguistic border between Miwok and Maidu. The diarist fails to mention the Mokelumne.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 25: northernmost extent of the expedition)

routes.label: Moraga — Southbound Return (Oct–Nov 1806)
routes.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions

stops.name: Cosumnes River — Turn South
stops.summary: Party turns south, staying close to the eastern edge of the valley along the Sierra foothills.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions

stops.name: Stanislaus / Tuolumne Area (return)
stops.summary: Passing back through the Stanislaus and Tuolumne country.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions

stops.name: Chowchilla / Mariposa Creek
stops.summary: Santo Domingo identified as Mariposa Cr.; Tecolote as the Chowchilla (Cutter).

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 26)

stops.name: Fresno River (Santa Ana)
stops.summary: The Santa Ana was the Fresno R.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 27)

stops.name: San Joaquin at Pizcache
stops.summary: Pizcache on the San Joaquin, listed in the appendix. Moraga probably halted approximately north of Fresno, below Le Grand. Distinct absence of permanent Indian settlements on seasonal streams.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 27)

stops.name: Pitkachi Village Area
stops.summary: Pitkachi 'held the south side of the San Joaquin, living at Kohou, near Herndon or Sycamore; at Weshiu, on a slough; and at Gewachu, still farther downstream.' Village was in the mountains or at least the foothills.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 28: Gayton / Kroeber disagree on exact location)

stops.name: Kings River — Near Sanger
stops.summary: Kings R. was reached near Sanger or Centerville.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 31)

stops.name: Kaweah Delta — Visalia / Telame
stops.summary: 28th day: scouts reached the great oak forest along the Kaweah delta at or above Visalia. 29th day (Oct. 19): main party arrived. The large village of 600 souls — Telame (the Telamni). 22 persons baptized.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 33: 'The water evidently was very low — somewhat unusual for this region, even in October')

stops.name: Tule River — Near Porterville
stops.summary: After a winding course for two days, the party camped on the Tule R. near the foothills, probably not far from Porterville.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 36)

stops.name: Deer Creek
stops.summary: Probably Deer Cr.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 37)

stops.name: White River
stops.summary: Probably White R.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 38)

stops.name: Poso Creek
stops.summary: No distances given, but from the description the most likely stream is Poso Cr.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 39)

stops.name: Kern River — Above Bakersfield
stops.summary: The Kern R., probably some miles above Bakersfield. A 'long trip' downstream brought them into the slough country south of Bakersfield.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 40)

stops.name: Kern River — Downstream / Slough Country
stops.summary: South of Bakersfield. Moraga did not get within sight of Buena Vista Lake, to the west.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook notes 40–41)

stops.name: Grapevine Canyon
stops.summary: Zalvidea had left an account of the villages in this area from his Aug. 1806 expedition.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 41)

stops.name: Tejon Pass
stops.summary: On the last three days the party left the valley by way of Grapevine Canyon, over Tejon Pass.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 42: 'Cutter says the Santa Susanna Range')

stops.name: Mission San Fernando
stops.summary: Arrival at San Fernando Mission. End of the 1806 expedition.

sources.citation: Muñoz/Zalvidea diaries via Cook, Colonial Expeditions (Cook note 42)
